subject: Cloud Computing
title: Data Centers Around the World
description: A quick, practical guide to Data Centers Around the World.

hook.text: Over 10,000 data centers exist worldwide. Some are the size of football stadiums.

buildup.text: Cloud providers build them near major populations to keep latency low for users.

discovery.text: A region contains multiple data centers called availability zones, built for redundancy.

twist.text: Many countries now require data about their citizens to stay within their borders.

climax.text: Building a data center costs billions of dollars, years of planning, and megawatts of power.

punchline.text: The cloud is physical. It just lives in someone else's building.

quiz.question: What are 'availability zones' in cloud computing?
options: Multiple data centers within a region, built for redundancy
Time zones where cloud services are available
Areas where internet speed is fast enough for streaming
